spec_match.rationale: The page exercises the HTML Standard Speculation-Rules response header surface with a restrictive CSP and multiple referenced rule documents.

interactive.rationale: Editable CSP and header values call the real local header route; the run button opens a top-level probe document and reports the server-recorded rules-document fetch.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page promises a CSP and Speculation-Rules header probe and now shows the emitted headers plus the observed Chrome rules-document request.

edge_cases.rationale: Verified a single rule document under script-src 'self' and two comma-separated rule documents under script-src 'none'.

design_system.rationale: Uses shared CSS variables, high-contrast status borders, and debounced input-driven route checks.

summary: Resolved the server-route follow-up. Chrome DevTools MCP on Chrome/148.0.0.0 verified the page at /v131/exempt-speculation-rules-header-from-csp-restrictions/spec-rules-csp/?verify=after-real-route: the route emitted real CSP and Speculation-Rules headers, the top-level probe recorded /rules.json, the multi-doc probe recorded /prefetch-rules.json and /prerender-rules.json, console was clean, and network showed only expected local route calls.
